text.title: LLM Hallucination Test Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Suite covers six pattern classes: fact_probes, grounding_required, refusal_correctness, citation_verification, contradiction_tests, off_topic_rejection. Missing any class rejected.
rule.rationale: Hallucination has multiple shapes; a single class misses the rest of the surface.

rule.statement: Each pattern class has ≥10 cases. Below 10, per-pattern pass-rate variance hides regressions.
rule.rationale: Per-pattern n<10 has so wide a CI that 20% regression looks like noise.

rule.statement: Each case has a gold label authored by a human, with explicit reasoning recorded. LLM-judge-only labels rejected.
rule.rationale: LLM judges drift; human-authored gold labels anchor the truth.

rule.statement: Each pattern has explicit pass/fail criteria documented in rubric (fact_probes pass = asserts correct fact OR refuses; refusal pass = refuses; citation pass = each cited source contains the claim). Ambiguous rubrics rejected.
rule.rationale: Pass/fail without rubric drifts case-by-case; rubric makes the suite reproducible.

rule.statement: Every hallucination-driven production incident produces ≥1 case within 7 days of postmortem, classified to one of six patterns.
rule.rationale: Production hallucination shapes differ from imagined ones; incident-derived cases catch what eng didn't predict.

rule.statement: Skip for non-LLM features (deterministic NLP), creative-content output, or when an existing eval framework already enforces hallucination coverage.
rule.rationale: Without an LLM in the loop, hallucination is not a failure mode.
